subject: [COMMON] Faster aafConfig template
commit_message:
[COMMON] Faster aafConfig template

"index" function is bad in term of performance in Helm.
Reworked the templates in order to avoid it.

as certificates are retrieved at every boot (and as already present
certs are deleted before), we don't need persistent storage

Also set aafImage as a global variable in order to have a consistent use
accross ONAP

Issue-ID: EXTAPI-375
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: Ie3f5ae5c2a37d816afc42d2c67ebe8e40e749c79

Patch Set 1:

"index" was requested by Krzystof, I cannot judge.
persistence: 
Fine with me. 
For some reasons aaf-hello example is using persistence
I interpreted this as a robustness option if aaf is not available and pods are restarting and should continue working.
Or'aaf' is not used at all and persistence volume can be used as an alternative way to provide certs.
BR, Alex

Patch Set 1:

Hi Alex,
It's not a "blame and shame" on you :). I just wanted to use your template and found some minor stuff to do!

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> "index" was requested by Krzystof, I cannot judge.

Krzysztof made a deep look at helm because we had this awful linting time and discovered that on tuesday so not your fault :)

> persistence: 
> Fine with me. 
> For some reasons aaf-hello example is using persistence
> I interpreted this as a robustness option if aaf is not available and pods are restarting and should continue working.
> Or'aaf' is not used at all and persistence volume can be used as an alternative way to provide certs.

That's what I thought but actually the script first destroy the certificates and __after__ tries to retrieve. So even when AAF is not working it won't be good...
Badly designed system...


> BR, Alex
